topics: Basics on Inorganic and Organic Semiconductors
Classification of materials based on electrical conductivity. Basic laws on electrical conductivity, Ohms law and Coulomb’s law. Valance band theory

basic concept of band model. Concept of doping. P-type and n-type doping and doping mechanism. P-n junctions. Oxidative and reductive dopants. Inorganic and organic dopants. Type of doping technique. Charge carriers polarons, bipolarons and solitons. Effect of doping on the properties of organic and organic semi
conductors. Effect of temperature on conductivity of inorganic and organic semiconductors

Historical Development and Synthesis of Important Conducting Polymers - Historical development of organic conductors. Basic structural characteristics of organic conductors. Methods of preparation of conducting polymers
electrochemical polymerization. Synthesis of organic conductors
poly(phenylene vinylene) and poly(vinyl carbazole). Conduction mechanism in organic conductors. Interchain and intrachain conduction
Properties and Processing of Conducting Polymers - Important properties of conducting polymers
dielectric loss and absorption properties. Processing of conducting polymers. Methods to enhance the process ability of conducting polymers. Advantages and disadvantages of conducting polymers
Applications of Conducting Polymers and Device Fabrication Methods - Applications of conducting polymers. Electroactive applications polymer rechargeable batteries
antistatic coatings and conductive adhesives. Electronic applications
EMI shielding and Frequency selective surface
